programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: After-School Recreation Care (ARC)
programs.description: Are you seeking recreational care at the end of the school day? After School Recreation Care (ARC) offers outdoor play, crafts, sports, games and physical literacy. Participants will receive homework help, nutritional education and a snack while focusing on social interactions and wellness.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Aquatic Fitness Conference and Instructor Recertification
programs.description: The City of Toronto and WaterArt International are cohosting a unique opportunity for anyone interested in aquatic fitness. The conference introduces new techniques and explores emerging trends in the aquatic fitness industry. Enjoy a variety of workshop sessions in both shallow and deep water, as well as theory workshops. The conference provides the opportunity for instructors to recertify their WaterArt qualifications through the two day workshops.Additionally, there is the opportunity to add a specialty certification to your resume. Skills will be taught through a combination of classroom and in-pool sessions. Practical and written examinations will be components of taking a specialty certification but is not a requirement for those wishing to attend the conference only.For more information on this activity, visit: https://www.toronto.ca/explore-enjoy/parks-recreation/program-activities/leadership-employment-readiness/aquatic-fitness-certifications/For employment with the City of Toronto, Standard First Aid CPR-C is required, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.

programs.name: Adapted CampTO Explore
programs.description: Adapted CampTO Explore is a fun and creative camp experience for campers with a special need or a disability. Enjoy modified active and quiet games, arts and crafts, sports and more while making new friends. A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

A workshop, a local trip or a visit from a special guest will add to the excitement.Participants must have a current AIS membership. For membership information contact the AIS Hotline at 416-395-6128 or email aiscw@toronto.ca.

programs.name: Junior Leader Training
programs.description: This course is designed for youth aged 14 to 17 who are interested in volunteering as a Junior Leader at the Expressions Summer Art Camp at Cedar Ridge. Leadership and basic arts skills will be developed during the program. Participants will receive a certificate confirming completion of the course. Limited volunteer positions available on a first come first served basis. This course is mandatory for all volunteers at Expressions. The minimum age requirement of 14 years is firm.

programs.name: Tennis: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills though play with recreational tennis. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Volleyball: Recreational
programs.description: Develop skills through play with recreational volleyball. Players of all levels can register to join the fun and participate in structured scrimmages or small-sided games where staff will ensure rules are followed and participation is balanced. There is no formal instruction.

programs.name: Extended Hours: AM
programs.description: This option provides supervised care before the start of the camp day for children registered in a City of Toronto Parks, Forestry and Recreation camp.Extended hours activities have a separate barcode for registration. Please note that campers must be registered at the same location for extended hours as the activity they have registered in. Refer to the camp program charts for times, fees and locations.
